title: Africa CDC Epidemic Services — Epidemiology Track Fellowship
description: A fully funded, two-year applied epidemiology training program run by the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC): three months of in-person didactic training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, followed by 21 months of applied training at a site hosted by an African Union Member State (placement location varies by fellow). Africa CDC covers travel-related costs, a stipend, hardware/software, and health insurance for the duration. Applicants must be citizens of an AU Member State, under 35 (per the AU youth definition), hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in a One Health-related field (medicine, veterinary medicine, nursing, environmental science, public health, or equivalent), have at least three years of recent full-time postgraduate public health experience in an African country, and secure a formal letter from their current employer granting leave for the two-year program with a guarantee of re-employment afterward. The application requires a valid AU-member-state passport copy, the employer release/guarantee letter, two professional references, certified degree certificates and transcripts, a CV on the mandatory AU template, and a 500-word motivation statement.
org: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC)
visaNote: Fellows relocate to Addis Ababa, Ethiopia for the three-month didactic phase and then to an assigned African Union Member State for the remaining 21 months; visa/residence arrangements are handled with programme support.

title: USDA Cochran Fellowship Program
description: A USDA Foreign Agricultural Service program that funds short-term, hands-on agricultural training in the United States for practicing professionals from selected emerging-market economies, covering agricultural trade, agribusiness development, management, policy, and marketing. Fellows are hosted by U.S. universities, government agencies, or private companies for roughly two to three weeks. The program is fully funded by USDA (international travel, lodging, and a per-diem allowance), with no cost to the fellow. Eligible countries vary each cycle depending on which USDA-approved training projects are funded; recent cycles have included Vietnam, Uzbekistan, Egypt, and other countries across North Africa and Southeast Asia — applicants should confirm current-cycle country eligibility with their nearest U.S. embassy Foreign Agricultural Service office or the FAS website, since not every nationality is eligible in every cycle. The FY2026 application deadline is August 14, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST.
org: USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (Cochran Fellowship Program)
visaNote: Fellows travel to the U.S. on a J-1 exchange visitor visa arranged with USDA/host-institution support; this is a training visa, not an employment-based work visa.
